$schema: Every session MUST update this file as its LAST action. Never edit fields of units you did not work on.

legend.rule: A phase's gate may be set to 'open' only when: all its units are 'verified' AND all reports in its gate_reports list exited 0.

last_session.unit: EIGHTEENTH SESSION: P2 INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION PASS. This session did not implement U-B.1/U-B.2/U-B.4 (prior sessions did) and did not touch Extractor.php, ConfigComponentWriter.php or backfill.php, so it is independent for those units per the self-certification convention. Substrate: scratch replica loaded from the 2026-07-22 13:43 production dump (fresh -- post-dual-write, post-AssetTag), run from a DISPOSABLE TREE at c:/tmp/ims-p2-verify so no edit could auto-upload to production. RESULT: run_all.php --gate P2 EXIT 0 -- equivalence/orphan/ledger/inventory ALL GREEN, plus schema GREEN and slot GREEN. Full test sweep 31/31 PASS, zero failures. U-B.1/U-B.2/U-B.4 implemented -> verified. GATE STAYS CLOSED, deliberately: those four reports are GREEN only on a replica with the NEW seeder 2026_07_22_004 applied. Against production as it stands today they are inventory RED(1) + equivalence RED(1). P2's gate opens the moment the owner applies that seeder and the reports are re-run against production -- no further machine-side work exists. THE ONE REMAINING DECISION WAS SETTLED WITH EVIDENCE: seeder 2026_07_21_001 section 3 left config 019eca1d's motherboard unassigned because the data looked ambiguous (board 55 was 'the only free unit' but server_name pointed at board 49). Two facts now resolve it to BOARD 49, and the '55 is the only free unit' argument is void because F-1 freed 49 as well -- which is the only reason 49 reads free today. (1) server_name IS 'DCMISGH631X7A5' = board 49's serial. (2) live config_components row 10016 is onboard NIC 'onboard-4c8f5e1b-49-1' (nicinventory 232, ParentInventoryID 49); that identity was minted by seeder 2026_07_20_002 section 1 whose UPDATE joins ON m.ServerUUID = n.ServerUUID, so it could only have attributed NIC 232 to board 49 if board 49 was itself bound to config 019eca1d at that moment -- i.e. on 2026-07-20 BEFORE F-1's delete freed boards 49/53/55 at 22:48:46. It is a snapshot of the pre-corruption binding, not a guess, and an onboard NIC cannot be in a server without the board it is soldered to. Board 55 has no record of any kind tying it to this config. Seeder 2026_07_22_004_settle-019eca1d-motherboard.sql re-binds board 49 + its onboard NIC, creates the missing config_components motherboard row, parents the onboard NIC row to it, adds the ledger provider rows mirroring b01a5f51 (same board model), and performs the half of F-3 that seeder 005 deliberately deferred (nic_config JSON onboard-4c8f5e1b-1 -> -49-1). Proven on the replica: all four gate reports go RED->GREEN, and a second run is a byte-stable no-op (config_components 76/76, config_resources 86/86). AWAITS MANUAL APPLICATION. VERIFICATION-OF-THE-VERIFIER (this repo has been burned by three probe errors): equivalence_report --self-test and inventory_report --self-test both PASS, i.e. both correctly detect a deliberately induced defect, so the GREENs are not vacuous. U-B.1 acceptance re-proved directly: --dry-run left CHECKSUM TABLE byte-identical on server_configurations/config_components/config_resources/config_events (provably write-free). U-B.4 acceptance: fleet dry-run is now 5 configs / 0 migrate / 0 quarantine, against 5 configs / 24 migrate / 50 quarantine at the twelfth session -- the fleet is fully backfilled with zero unexplained quarantines. U-B.2 acceptance: equivalence_report --config exit 0 individually for all 5 production configs. TWO STALE-FIXTURE TEST BUGS FOUND AND FIXED (test files only, never deployed; neither is a product defect): F-6 tests/unit/target_state_test.php asserted source=json for every component on the premise that config_components was EMPTY for fixture 06ea5abb -- U-B.4's backfill made that false (13 live rows, ids 10017-10082), so fromCurrent() correctly takes the ROWS path and the test failed 15 assertions against correct code. TargetStateBuilder's own docblock lines 21-22 already said the rows path becomes universal once the backfill lands. Rewritten to clear the rows inside a rolled-back transaction (leaf-first, fk_cc_parent is RESTRICT and the tree is 2 deep) so BOTH paths are exercised deterministically; also offset the synthetic inventory_ids by 900000 because uq_inventory_once is (inventory_table, inventory_id) GLOBALLY and a bare abs(id) could make insert()'s upsert steal another config's row. F-7b tests/regression/finalize_command_test.php hardcoded created_by=5, a users.id that no longer exists (live ids 2,4,38,39,43,44,46,52), so every fixture INSERT died on fk_server_config_user instead of testing anything; now resolves a real id. NEW FINDING F-7 -- loadEnvFile() OVERRIDES THE PROCESS ENVIRONMENT (core/config/app.php:25-58 putenv()s every key unconditionally). This is contrary to standard dotenv semantics and it silently broke tests/backfill/ledger_backfill_test.php: the test putenv()s IMS_DATA_PATH to a temp fixture spec dir and passes it into the backfill subprocess, but the subprocess's own app.php bootstrap overwrote it with .env's value, so the fixture motherboard spec was 'not found' and all 20 assertions failed against correct code. Proved by removing IMS_DATA_PATH from the scratch .env: ALL PASS. NOT FIXED THIS SESSION BY CHOICE -- app.php is a production bootstrap that auto-deploys on save, and changing env precedence mid-verification is exactly the kind of unrelated risk a verification pass should not take. Recommended fix (owner's call): only putenv() a key when getenv() does not already return a value. Same root cause also explains why DB_NAME could not be steered by env var, which is why this session used a disposable tree with its own .env. STILL BLOCKING FULL CUTOVER, unchanged and NOT time-compressible by this session: P3's STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED=shadow flag flip has NEVER been performed -- the flag still reads off/raw_seen=null -- so P3's 7-day soak clock has not started; U-C.6's enforce soak cannot begin until shadow lands (sequential, not parallel); and F-5 (parent_id coverage divergence between backfilled and live-written rows) still needs an owner decision before enforce. The .env write must be done by the owner through the VS Code SFTP extension: curl/FTP APPE was proven in the fifteenth session to revert within 1-2 minutes.
last_session.seeder_applied_20260722: OWNER APPLIED seeder 2026_07_22_004 to production 2026-07-22 (phpMyAdmin, all statements green) and synced code. CONFIRMED LIVE via read-only API probes: (1) motherboardinventory 49 = Status 2 / status_v2 installed / ServerUUID 019eca1d (was Status 1 / NULL) -- resolves inventory_report's referenced_while_available; board 53 still bound to b01a5f51 and board 55 still free, i.e. no collateral. (2) config_components row 10090 exists = motherboard / motherboardinventory 49 / spec 4c8f5e1b / serial DCMISGH631X7A5, matching phpMyAdmin's reported insert id. (3) row 10016 (onboard NIC, nicinventory 232) now has parent_id = 10090 -- section 4 landed. (4) server-get-config reports nic uuid onboard-4c8f5e1b-49-1 and motherboard serial DCMISGH631X7A5, so the legacy JSON and the rows now AGREE on both components -- section 6 landed, which is exactly what equivalence_report was RED on. Section 5 (config_resources provider rows) has no read-only API surface and was not directly observed, but it necessarily executed: phpMyAdmin runs statements sequentially and section 6, which follows it, demonstrably took effect. PROBE NOTE for future sessions: server-debug-config-dualwrite returns config_components / config_events as {count, rows} objects, NOT bare arrays -- count() over them yields 2 and reads as catastrophic data loss. Caught here only because a known-good control config returned the same 2. Always run that action through a control row.
last_session.gates_changed: P2 units all -> verified; P2 gate REMAINS CLOSED pending one thing only: a production dump exported AFTER the seeder, to re-run run_all.php --gate P2 against it (U-B.4's literal criterion is 'exit 0 on production replica'). Gate exit 0 is already proven on a replica of the pre-seeder dump WITH the seeder applied, and all four of the specific violations are confirmed fixed live via API.
last_session.superseded_seventeenth_session: SEVENTEENTH SESSION: server-compat removal audit + U-C.3 hardening. Audit findings reported to owner: production (COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED=off) still runs legacy removeComponent(), whose only dependency check is nic->sfp -- motherboard removal strands cpu/ram/cards silently (the owner's reported bug; the built-but-dormant DependencyBlockedRemovalRule+cascade already covers it once enforce lands). CODE CHANGES (all deployed via git branch, not run against production this session): (1) RemoveComponentCommand::apply() release fail-closed -- ports legacy removeComponent()'s 2026-07-21 hardening the command layer was missing: serial-less rows (motherboard/chassis/hbacard scalar-column types) resolve their physical unit via the inventory row's ServerUUID binding; zero bound units = stale entry, release skipped with log (cleanup proceeds); a release returning false (ambiguity refusal) now throws CommandFailed('unit_release_failed',409) rolling back the whole command, instead of the previous ignored return value that would tombstone the config row while leaving the unit Status=2 with a stale ServerUUID (the exact motherboardinventory-#45 leak observed live 2026-07-21 on the legacy path, reintroduced here under enforce+cascade for every serial-less row). (2) RemoveComponentCommand::cascadeRemovedRows() accessor + handleRemoveComponent enforce path now returns cascade_removed rows on success and a retry-with-cascade=true hint on dependency_blocked (both additive keys, absent in the non-cascade golden shape). (3) DependencyBlockedRemovalRule docblock parent_id claim corrected (stale since BOARD_HOSTED_TYPES/2026_07_21_002 -- said only sfp->nic populates parent_id). U-C.3 verified -> implemented (its verified code changed; self-certification rule). OPEN FINDINGS handed to owner, no code change: chassis is un-cascade-removable by construction (caddy/storage/motherboard carry parent_id NULL so cascade removes nothing and mechanism 2 blocks -- needs a semantics decision: parent chassis-hosted types or extend cascade to type-level dependents); storage survives motherboard cascade via the rule's documented type-level simplification (chassis stands in for backplane); cpu.requires_board E->VF relaxation worth a re-decision; command shadow JSONL still unconsumed by parity_report; the dormant constraint-state effort (tasks/todo.md Phase 0-5) overlaps this migration and should be explicitly retired. Tests: php -l clean on all 4 touched files; remove/add_command_test + both tests/api files DB-free portions ALL PASS (DB scenarios SKIPPED -- no scratch DB or ims-data in this environment; dependency_rule_test needs ims-data, dual_write_test's known non-self-skip DB fatal unchanged). The new fail-closed release path has NOT been executed against a live DB -- a scratch-DB session must add/run a release-failure scenario in remove_command_test.php before U-C.3 re-verifies.
last_session.superseded_seventeenth_gates_changed: none -- P6 stays closed (U-C.3 back to implemented, U-C.6 still blocked)

sixteenth_session_2026_07_21.unit: SIXTEENTH SESSION: U-B.4 soak signoff. Probed production via server-debug-migration-flags: DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED=on with raw_seen='on', .env mtime 2026-07-19T11:30:50Z UNCHANGED across 41h21m (verified 2026-07-21T04:51:45Z) -- zero reverts, clearing the >=24h soak requirement and confirming the 15th session's conclusion that the earlier reverts were the curl/FTP-APPE append method, not an external reverting process. Real-traffic evidence (the soak's actual point, not another synthetic probe): config 2d66d58f was exercised through the UI on 2026-07-20 17:18 UTC during unrelated onboard-NIC work and produced 4 correct config_events (revisions 1-4, add/remove/add/remove, motherboard, no gaps) plus 1 config_components row correctly tombstoned on removal. Three candidate anomalies checked and cleared: (1) config bc90e06e created in-window with zero dual-write rows is an EMPTY config -- hooks fire on component add/remove (ServerBuilder.php:885,:1134), not config creation, so zero is correct; (2) actor=0 on all events is deliberate and documented at ServerBuilder.php:894 (no authenticated-user context in ServerBuilder today) -- revisit when the command layer owns the write path; (3) 2d66d58f/b01a5f51 updated_at inside the window came from the 2026-07-20 seeder's direct SQL, not app writes, so no dual-write rows were expected. No engine exceptions, no FK violations, no partial writes; the 15th session's deleteConfiguration() FK fix did not regress. U-B.4 blocked -> implemented (self-certification convention: same effort implemented and tested it). CORRECTION worth carrying: closing U-B.4 does NOT open the P2 gate -- P2 still holds U-B.1 and U-B.2 at 'implemented' and U-B.4 is now 'implemented' too, and this file's own rule requires ALL units 'verified'. What actually changed is that P2 has no BLOCKED units left. The remaining P2 precondition is an independent verification pass over U-B.1/U-B.2/U-B.4 by someone other than the implementer, plus a re-run of the four gate_reports (equivalence/orphan/ledger/inventory) which were last GREEN on 2026-07-15 -- before dual-write had ever genuinely fired in production, so the conditions they were blessed under no longer hold. That pass is NOT time-gated and can run in parallel with the P3 soak. DEVIATION from tasks/todo.md step 20: both diagnostic actions (server-debug-migration-flags, server-debug-config-dualwrite) STAY DEPLOYED rather than being removed now -- two flag flips remain (STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED=shadow for P3, COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED shadow->enforce for U-C.6) on the same .env that took ~3 weeks and several sessions before a write stuck, and these read-only actions are the only observability into what the live process reads. Remove them at U-C.6/P9 cleanup once the last flip is confirmed held. Also this session: added _note_onboard_nic_fail_closed to scripts/verify/expected_diffs.json recording the AddComponentCommand fail-closed onboard-NIC divergence (a precondition for COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED=enforce) -- filed as a _note_* key rather than an entries row because entries is strictly the ENGINE_MODE rule-diff matcher keyed on rule_id (parity_report.php:56) and this is a command-layer side effect emitting no rule_id in a different JSONL stream, so a fabricated entries row would never match; same posture as _note_dependency_blocked_removal. Critical path unchanged otherwise: P3 needs a 7-day STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED=shadow soak whose clock has NOT started (flag reads off/raw_seen=null), then U-C.6 needs a 7-day enforce soak that cannot begin until shadow lands -- sequential, not parallel. Floor to full cutover ~3 weeks assuming zero findings. Signoff: migration/handoffs/U-B.4-SIGNOFF-20260721.md.
sixteenth_session_2026_07_21.gates_changed: none -- P2 stays closed (units not all verified)

fifteenth_session_2026_07_19.unit: FIFTEENTH SESSION: U-B.4 re-diagnosis. Owner ran the 13th session's verification SQL again against a NEW throwaway config (0477956c-e230-424b-afa3-e5d166aaff3a, motherboard add/remove) after attesting (2026-07-16) that .env was fixed and the service restarted -- config_events/config_components STILL EMPTY, same symptom as before. Built a temporary read-only diagnostic API action (server-debug-migration-flags, api/handlers/server/server_api.php::handleDebugMigrationFlags(), admin/super_admin-gated on top of the server.view ACL entry in permission_map.php) that reports what the LIVE PHP process reads for all 5 FLAGS.md flags via the exact getenv->$_ENV->default->whitelist pattern every flag class uses, plus .env found/mtime -- deliberately never reads .env content itself (hard rule intact). NOTE: the auto-upload-on-save FTP watcher did not pick up this session's 2 file edits (6 consecutive 'Unknown operation' responses over ~40s) -- worked around with a one-off manual FTP push of just those 2 files using the credentials already in ims-ftp/.vscode/sftp.json; flagged to the owner as a possible standing gap in the save-to-deploy workflow, not assumed to be a one-off fluke. ROOT CAUSE FOUND via the live probe: all 5 flags read 'off' with raw_seen=null (the keys are ABSENT from the .env this process loads, not just wrong-valued), and env_file.mtime = 2026-06-25T11:25:23Z -- three-plus weeks before BOTH the owner's 2026-07-14 'set DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED=on' and 2026-07-16 'fixed + restarted' attestations. The file the live app actually loads has not changed since before either of those actions, meaning the owner's edits are landing in the wrong file/path/docroot, or not saving -- a service restart was never going to fix this since the process was never the problem. CONTINUATION (same session): owner authorized a blind FTP append (payload was a leading-newline + 'DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED=on' only -- .env content was never downloaded or read, hard rule intact) to the confirmed path. Immediately re-probed: flag flipped to 'on', fresh mtime -- the write genuinely landed. Built a second diagnostic action, server-debug-config-dualwrite (same file/gating pattern), to read config_events/config_components for one config_uuid over the API instead of a manual SQL paste. Ran a brand-new throwaway add/remove test (config ac3c472f-3d91-4b21-a9f4-edd44ef2244b, motherboard d8e9f0a1... serial 2M20200BJC) seconds after confirming the flag was on -- legacy side succeeded as always, but dual-write rows were STILL EMPTY. Re-checked server-debug-migration-flags immediately after: the flag had REVERTED to 'off', mtime back to the exact prior 2026-06-25T11:25:23Z (confirmed via a byte-diff of the app code itself, which is unchanged -- ruled out as a code bug). 20 further rapid probes all show the same reverted state consistently, not flapping between two values, which argues against simple multi-node load-balancer inconsistency and toward something actively restoring the file within 1-2 minutes of any edit (leading hypothesis: a cPanel/CloudLinux security tool like Imunify360's file-integrity/Proactive-Defense reverting an 'unauthorized' change to a sensitive file, or a cron-based config-sync job -- unconfirmed, needs the owner to check hosting-side logs/cron). This is very likely why BOTH the owner's 2026-07-14 and 2026-07-16 fix attempts silently failed to stick -- now directly observed in real time rather than inferred. Cleaned up the throwaway config (component removed, config deleted) before ending. FURTHER CONTINUATION (same session) -- RESOLVED: owner ruled out cron/monitoring/deploy-pipeline and does not want any of those set up. Revised theory: the revert was `curl --append` (FTP APPE) not committing reliably on this FTP server, not an external reverting process. Owner added DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED=on manually via the VS Code SFTP extension (the same path used for every other deploy this whole project) and pasted their own SFTP-pulled copy of the live .env, which independently (via a completely different access path than the diagnostic tool) confirmed the flag was absent at that moment -- cross-confirmation, not a contradiction. Verified via server-debug-migration-flags immediately after the manual save (on, fresh mtime) and again ~2.5 minutes later (SAME mtime, still on) -- held this time, confirming the append method was the actual weak link, not an external process. Ran a full add/remove cycle (config 8c5684a2-c3c0-4093-af19-76cd93d703f3, motherboard d8e9f0a1... serial 2M20200BJC): server-debug-config-dualwrite showed a REAL config_events row on add (revision 1), a REAL config_components row, a second REAL config_events row on remove (revision 2), and correct tombstoning (removed_at set, row kept not deleted) -- dual-write is genuinely firing end-to-end for the first time since U-1.5 was written weeks ago. NEW BUG FOUND + FIXED: cleanup via server-delete-config failed with a real FK violation -- ServerBuilder::deleteConfiguration() (core/models/server/ServerBuilder.php) predates the U-1.5/U-L.2 dual-write tables and never cleaned up config_resources/config_events/config_components before deleting the parent server_configurations row; never surfaced before because dual-write had never actually fired in production until this session, so under DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED=off (P1's own verified condition) those tables were always empty for any deleted config and the missing cleanup was unreachable -- not a P1 regression, a P2-scoped gap only reachable once the flag is genuinely on. Fixed: config_resources deleted first (fk_cr_consumer is ON DELETE RESTRICT against config_components), then config_events, then config_components, before the existing history/parent deletes. Deployed via manual FTP STOR (not append), verified byte-identical against the server copy, retested -- delete now succeeds cleanly. This would have broken server.delete for any real config going forward now that the flag is genuinely on; good this surfaced on a throwaway test. Cleaned up both throwaway configs (this session's 8c5684a2-... and the older 0477956c-... left over from a prior session, zero dual-write rows, safe no-op). Production has no leftover test debris. U-B.4's dual-write MECHANISM is now confirmed genuinely working for the first time -- but the 24h real-traffic soak clock only starts now (~2026-07-19 11:37 UTC), same as if this were day zero; do not treat U-B.4 as done yet. No unit_status or gate changed this session (deleteConfiguration fix is a genuine bug fix, implemented not verified, self-certification applies). Both temporary debug actions (server-debug-migration-flags, server-debug-config-dualwrite) stay deployed for the remaining soak monitoring; remove both once U-B.4 closes. See tasks/todo.md steps 12-20 for the full account.
fifteenth_session_2026_07_19.handoff_file: tasks/todo.md (steps 10-20); migration/handoffs/SESSION-20260712-FINDINGS-ABC.md not yet appended this session

thirteenth_session_2026_07_15.unit: THIRTEENTH SESSION (continuation, evening): backfill data verified GREEN against REAL production data (loaded owner's post-seeder dump into a fresh replica, all 5 gate reports GREEN, row counts match rehearsal exactly). Dual-write soak evidence CONFIRMED NOT MET (not just unconfirmed): owner's read-only SELECTs for the throwaway test config both came back empty despite the legacy write succeeding cleanly -- code-level analysis (ServerBuilder's single outer catch would have rolled back the legacy write too if the dual-write call had thrown) proves DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED is not actually 'on' as read by the production PHP process, contradicting the owner's 2026-07-14 attestation. This session modified live code in ConfigComponentWriter.php (deferred psu_watt consumption + retro-attach, fixing a live production regression where adding any power-consuming component before the chassis rolled back the whole add), ResourceCatalog.php (consumesStorage() lane-count parity with legacy, was over-counting M.2-adjacent NVMe), and Extractor.php/backfill.php (''->NULL slot_ref normalization, F-PSU skip-with-log) -- downgrading U-B.1/U-B.2/U-L.1/U-L.2 from verified to implemented per this repo's self-certification rule (this session both wrote and ran the tests for these). Both seeders (2026_07_15_001 placeholder inventory, 2026_07_15_002 backfill rows) run on production by the owner and confirmed idempotent/correct. U-B.4 stays blocked -- now on a concrete, diagnosable owner action (check production ims-ftp/.env for DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED, and whether the cPanel/CloudLinux host needs a PHP-FPM/LiteSpeed restart after .env edits) instead of a vague 'wait longer'. See handoff for full account.

twelfth_session_2026_07_15.unit: TWELFTH SESSION: U-B.4 first real-fleet backfill attempt against a FRESH production replica (ims_prod_replica, dump generated 2026-07-14 19:05, golden DB untouched/backed up). Owner actions preceding the session: DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED=on in production ~24h (soak precondition MET, attested), permission seeders run (confirmed via read-only API probe: server.finalize/replace/transition rows exist; finalize edges from 2026_07_13_001 present in dump), COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED still off (probe: v2 actions 403). New CPU spec E5-2640 v3 added by owner to ims-data; mirrors re-synced. RESULT: dry-run 5 configs / 24 migrate / 50 quarantine (ALL ambiguous-serial); --execute on replica run-20260714-192824-9c9f01 done=0 quarantined=4 error=1 (NEW finding F-PSU: chassis-less config b01a5f51 has no psu_watt provider for CPU consumption -- ledger backfill hard-fails; live AddComponentCommand ordering question flagged). Post-backfill gate reports all RED, every cause = production data quality (serial-less quantity JSON entries with missing/unlinked inventory rows; 12 orphan inventory_missing; pciecard 07dc91dd referenced-while-available), NOT engine defects. SOAK CAVEAT: dump proves zero config mutations in production since 2026-04-21 -- dual-write hook has never actually fired there; controlled test mutation was permission-blocked this session, handed to owner as an explicit step. U-B.4 stays blocked -- now on OWNER DATA WORK (serialize inventory or prune phantom JSON entries; see triage doc). No unit_status or gate changed; no production write; production .env never read.
twelfth_session_2026_07_15.handoff_file: migration/handoffs/SESSION-20260712-FINDINGS-ABC.md (appended twelfth-session section) + migration/07-component-migration/UB4-QUARANTINE-TRIAGE-20260715.md (full per-entry triage + owner decision list)

independent_verify_2026_07_13_over_eleve.unit: INDEPENDENT VERIFY over the eleventh-session batch: both changes confirmed (fixture_scenarios_real.php PROBE_DB_* self-skip reproduced -- honest SKIPPED + exit 0, probe logic untouched; MANIFEST.md spot-checked exact against the tree). Full sweep reproduced: 38/38 exit 0 without harness against the manifest list, both api files ALL PASS with own harness, fleet sweep 84/9 unexplained=0 GREEN, characterization zero drift, gate reports unchanged (schema/ledger/slot GREEN; equivalence/orphan/inventory/performance RED known; parity bare RED / run_all --gate P4 exit 0), all seeders confirmed unrun via DB. NO new findings; no unit/gate changes. NO further implementable work remains until an owner action lands (seeders, perf re-bless, gate authorization, soaks). See verify record in handoff.
independent_verify_2026_07_13_over_eleve.handoff_file: migration/handoffs/SESSION-20260712-FINDINGS-ABC.md (appended verify record over the eleventh-session batch)
independent_verify_2026_07_13_over_eleve.superseded_note: ELEVENTH SESSION: fixed the tenth-session verify's reporting-accuracy finding at the root -- tests/fixture_scenarios_real.php converted to the same env-gated self-skip convention as tests/regression/_scratch_db.php (PROBE_DB_* env vars, default imsbdcmsbharatda_Ims_Production, prints SKIPPED + exit 0 instead of hard-fataling with an uncaught PDOException when that local production-dump mirror isn't reachable); probe logic itself untouched. Wrote tests/MANIFEST.md enumerating the canonical sweep exactly: 30 *_test.php across 5 dirs (tests/unit 8, tests/unit/rules 8, tests/regression 10, tests/api 2, tests/backfill 2) + 8 named top-level legacy scripts (5 authority units, serverstate_equivalence, getDashboardDataShapeTest, fixture_scenarios_real) = 38, with tests/state_machine_unit.php (never run against ims_compat_golden, standing rule) and characterize_compatibility.php/fleet_parity_sweep.php/scripts/verify/*_report.php (tools, not tests) explicitly excluded from that count. Re-ran the full sweep against this manifest: 30/30 canonical exit 0 without the HTTP harness; 8/8 legacy exit 0 (fixture_scenarios_real.php now SKIPPED cleanly instead of fataling) -- 38/38. With a scratch-only HTTP harness (127.0.0.1:8099, COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED=enforce process env) both tests/api files' previously-SKIPPED DB+HTTP criteria ALL PASS -- 38/38 again. fleet_parity_sweep: 84/9/0-threw, identical=47 expected=37 unexplained=0 GREEN (byte-matches every prior session). Characterization: regenerated md5 byte-identical to checked-in baseline (zero drift, restore a genuine no-op). Gate reports re-run: schema/ledger/slot GREEN, equivalence/orphan/inventory RED (known pre-U-B.4 state, unchanged), performance RED (same baseline-variance pattern, unchanged), parity bare RED / run_all --gate GREEN via --since (unchanged). DB residue: 9 configs, 12 transitions, 0 server.finalize permission rows, viewer still holds server.edit -- confirms no seeder was run. No unit_status or gate changed -- this session both implemented and ran everything itself (self-certification, per this repo's own convention); independent verification is still needed. independent_verify_2026_07_13_over_eleve.superseded_note_tenth: TENTH SESSION: owner decisions executed for two of the ninth-session's open items. ninth_session_2026_07_13.note: (1) SERVER.FINALIZE SEEDER: database/seeders/2026_07_13_002_add-server-finalize-permission.sql, mirrors 2026_07_12_001's shape exactly -- one permissions row + role_permissions grants copied from whichever roles hold server.edit today (confirmed by direct query: admin/super_admin/viewer), idempotent, NOT run against any DB this session. (2) FINDING 2 FIXED (verify-finding, eighth-session verify record): finalize_command_test.php's chain-walk assertion was dead code for two stacked reasons -- actor 0 (no grants) failed the very first edge, AND even with a real actor the original loop bound walked all the way into 'finalized' via applyConfigTransition(), which would have made the subsequent explicit dryRun() hit a nonexistent finalized->finalized self-transition. Fixed by using actor 38 (superadmin, same id the file's own Finding 2 scenario already uses) for the loop's transitions and changing the loop bound to stop at 'validated', leaving the pre-existing (U-SM.2) validated->finalized edge for the dryRun() call; that call and the defective-inventory scenario right after it now distinguish a tolerated CommandFailed (the separate server.finalize permission gap) from a genuine no-such-transition failure instead of asserting blind pass/fail. Executed proof: the assertion now genuinely runs and PASSES against the scratch DB (rolled back). (3) FINDING 1 FIXED (verify-finding): parity_report.php gained an opt-in --since YYYY-MM-DD flag (default omitted = original behavior, unchanged -- confirmed a no-args run still reports RED); scripts/verify/prune_shadow_log.php written as the file-log equivalent of a seeder (dry-run by default, needs --execute to write, never run with --execute this session against either tree -- a dry run against the real tree reported kept=0 pruned=199, matching the RED row count exactly, files left byte-unchanged). Proof: --since 2026-07-13 against the scratch tree's own already-accumulated 07-13 shadow rows produced a genuine non-zero-sample GREEN (170 ops, unexplained=0); against the real tree (no 07-13 file yet) it correctly produces a zero-sample warning-GREEN. Owner decision still open: adopt --since as the gate's standing invocation, run the prune, or both -- no gate was flipped by this fix, since the default (no --since) invocation is still what the board's own rule reads and it stays RED. (4) EVERY GATE REPORT RE-RUN from the scratch tree: schema/ledger/slot GREEN; equivalence/orphan/inventory RED (known pre-U-B.4 state, unchanged); performance RED (NEW observation -- +3499%/p95 on tiny 2-8 sample counts, reads as scratch-machine timing variance rather than a real regression since no code this session touched sits on the add/finalize hot path -- flagged, not investigated further, out of scope); parity RED on default invocation, GREEN with --since (see (3)). (5) FULL SWEEP: 30/30 test files exit 0 both WITHOUT and WITH the scratch-only HTTP harness (COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED=enforce as a process env var only, reused an already-running harness server from earlier in this session after confirming it reflected current code); fleet_parity_sweep.php 84 replays/9 configs/0 threw/identical=47/expected=37/unexplained=0/GREEN; characterize_compatibility.php byte-identical to the checked-in 9/84 baseline (zero drift, restore was a no-op); post-sweep DB residue check: 9 configs, 0 leftover fixture rows. Production .env never read/touched; all flags stayed off/unset in production throughout. Everything this session: implemented, never verified -- no unit_status or gate changed. Still open: both new/carried seeders (2026_07_13_001, 2026_07_13_002) awaiting owner execution, the --since/prune owner decision, the new performance_report RED observation, U-C.6/U-B.4/P2/P3 soaks (unchanged, human/time-gated), U-D.1-U-D.4 all not_started (gated behind P8/P9).

eighth_session_2026_07_13.note: (1) FINDING 2 FIXED (owner-authorized): config_status_transitions only had ONE edge into 'finalized' (validated->finalized), forcing the full draft->building->validating->validated->finalized chain -- legacy ServerBuilder::finalizeConfiguration() has NO such status_v2 precondition when StateGuard is off (production default), so a real fleet config sitting at draft/building (where production configs are actually found) would be silently BLOCKED from finalizing the moment COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED flips to enforce, a regression vs legacy. Wrote database/seeders/2026_07_13_001_add-finding2-finalize-edges.sql (2 new rows: draft->finalized, building->finalized, same server.finalize/full as the existing edge) -- shown, NOT run against any DB this session, scratch included, per standing rule (a permission-classifier boundary confirmed this rule is enforced literally even for the scratch DB). Proved the fix WITHOUT running the seeder: finalize_command_test.php's new Finding-2 scenario inserts the 2 edge rows as its OWN fixture inside a rolled-back transaction (same pattern every other DB fixture in this file uses), asserts via StateMachine::assertConfigTransition() directly (isolates edge-existence from the separate ACL-permission question) that draft/building->finalized no longer reads 'no such transition', plus a control assertion that an edge NOT added (draft->validated) still correctly stays blocked. NEW FINDING surfaced while writing this scenario: 'server.finalize' -- the permission EVERY edge into 'finalized' requires, including the pre-existing validated->finalized edge from U-SM.2 -- does not exist as a row in the `permissions` table at all in the scratch DB, and no role has it granted; every finalize-bound transition is therefore currently permission-unsatisfiable for any actor, including superadmin. This is a separate, pre-existing gap this fix does not touch (not in Finding 2's scope) -- flagged for owner decision (real permission-seeding gap vs. seeder oversight from whenever U-SM.2 landed). (2) HTTP HARNESS BUILT (tests/api/_http_harness.php, self-skips via IMS_HTTP_HARNESS_URL env var same convention as scratch_db_connect()) and wired into add_remove_response_shape_test.php + new_actions_test.php's previously-unconditionally-SKIPPED lines. Ran a scratch-only php -S 127.0.0.1:8099 -t <scratch tree> server with COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED=enforce set ONLY as a process env var for that one server process (never written to any .env, scratch or production). All 4 criteria named in the instruction now PASS for the first time over real HTTP: golden response-shape (X-IMS-Deprecation header + exact legacy envelope field set, byte-verified), 409-with-real-current_revision after a genuine committed mutation, serial-targeted remove (2 same-spec-UUID units, removes the exact targeted serial, the other stays attached), and the two v2 actions (replace happy [storage A->B, both real co-resident specs from an actual fleet config] + replace blocked [chassis A/B real bay-capacity mismatch, same pair replace_command_test.php already proves in-process] + transition legal [draft->building->validating->validated, a fully-equipped fixture config walking real existing edges] + transition illegal [empty config draft->building, blocked by system.required_set, 422]). Found and fixed 2 real bugs in the harness fixture code along the way (both test-file-only, no core/ touched): (a) HttpHarness::connect() destructured a 3-element rawPost() return into 2 variables, silently assigning the headers array where the JSON body was expected -- always failed login; (b) h_attachComponent() read lastInsertId() AFTER an intervening UPDATE statement, occasionally returning 0 -- reordered to capture it immediately post-INSERT. Also fixed a config_components self-referencing-FK teardown ordering bug (the onboard-NIC row's parent_id pointed at the motherboard row in the same DELETE's WHERE-matched set) by deleting child rows (parent_id IS NOT NULL) before the rest. U-A.2 STAYS 'implemented', not promoted to 'verified' -- this session wrote AND ran these criteria in the same sitting, which is self-certification by this repo's own explicit rule, not independent verification. (3) DUAL-WRITE SOAK MONITORING: migration/07-component-migration/DUAL-WRITE-SOAK-MONITORING.md (owner runbook: cadence, alert-condition table) + scripts/verify/dual_write_soak_monitor.php (read-only: runs P2's 4 gate reports via the standard app.php bootstrap against whatever DB the deployed .env points at, plus a NEW staleness check comparing config_events vs config_components activity in a recent window to catch 'flag says on but nothing landing'). fleet_parity_sweep.php deliberately excluded from this script -- confirmed it's a scratch-DB-only replay tool (GOLDEN_DB_* env, needs a full ims-data mirror), never designed for a live database; documented as an optional secondary scratch-refresh check instead. Tested against the scratch DB: correctly reported RED on equivalence/orphan/inventory (matches the well-documented, pre-existing 'fleet is json-fallback-only pre-U-B.4' state -- not a new problem, a confirmatory result that the script works), GREEN on ledger, staleness correctly INCONCLUSIVE (no mutation activity in the test window). (4) U-X.1 PLAN RE-VERIFIED (migration/09-cutover/U-X.1-PLAN-20260712.md): every citation re-derived against current ServerBuilder.php -- zero further drift since 2026-07-12 (getConfigurationDetails still 2248, getConfigComponents still 5106, still exactly one call site, ConfigComponentRepository::liveRows() still present) -- gate chain unchanged, P8 stays not_started. (5) U-D.2/U-D.3/U-D.4 PLANNING DOCS WRITTEN (migration/10-cleanup/U-D.{2,3,4}-PLAN-20260713.md), planning only, matching U-D.1's precedent: U-D.2 spot-checked several citations (validateConfiguration 3166->3266, validateConfigurationEnhanced 3275->3375, getConfigurationWarnings 1875->1974, validateConfigurationComprehensive 6414->6681, api call sites +329/+340 drift) and confirmed the 5 authority classes + OnboardNICHandler::replaceOnboardNIC still present; U-D.3 (the point-of-no-return unit) explicitly did NOT get a seeder or backup attempt -- both would be stale by the time its 30-day-signoff-plus preconditions actually clear, reasoned through explicitly rather than just deferred; U-D.4 confirmed both TEMP-GUARD blocks + all 4 legacy authority-era flags still present, matching the pack's own 'their consumers die in U-D.2 first' framing. All three stay not_started; the strict U-D.1->U-D.4 order and P8/P9 gate chain are unaffected by any of this. (6) FULL SWEEP RE-RUN: 30/30 test files exit 0 (all tests/unit, tests/unit/rules, tests/regression, tests/api including the newly-unSKIPPED HTTP scenarios, tests/backfill), fleet_parity_sweep.php 84 replays/9 configs/0 threw/identical=47/expected=37/unexplained=0/GREEN, characterize_compatibility.php regenerated and byte-identical to the checked-in 9/84 baseline (zero drift, no restore needed). Production .env never read/touched; ENGINE_MODE/STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED/COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED/DUAL_WRITE_ENABLED/READ_FROM_ROWS all stayed off/unset in production throughout -- COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED=enforce existed ONLY as a process env var for the scratch-only php -S harness server, never in any .env file. No seeder run (Finding 2's shown, not run). Everything this session: implemented, never verified -- no unit_status promoted, no gate opened. A mid-session tooling outage (php.exe/mysql.exe execution unavailable for an extended stretch, matching the exact pattern the fifth session documented on 2026-07-12) hit partway through -- unlike that session, this one recovered and completed all remaining work once execution came back; mysqld and the harness server both had to be restarted after the outage (both die with the process that started them, a now well-documented environment characteristic, not a new issue). Still open: the new server.finalize permission-seeding gap (owner decision), Finding 2's seeder itself still needs to actually be run by the owner for the fix to take effect anywhere, U-C.6/U-B.4/P2/P3 soak preconditions (unchanged, human/time-gated), U-D.1-U-D.4 all not_started (gated behind P8/P9 as documented in each plan).

seventh_session_2026_07_13.unit: RV-4 fix (ResourceCatalog::consumesStorage() M.2 lane exclusion, U-L.1/U-L.2) + scratch golden DB ghost-config cleanup (owner decision executed) + seeder 2026_07_12_002 marked superseded + full sweep re-run + a genuine test-infra bug fix (ledger_backfill_test.php subprocess env)
seventh_session_2026_07_13.note: RV-4 (carried from the 2026-07-12 VERIFY-ALL audit, re-confirmed live by the 2026-07-13 verify session): ResourceCatalog::consumesStorage() (core/models/config/ResourceCatalog.php) did not exclude M.2 NVMe from pcie_lane consumption, unlike legacy PcieLaneBudgetValidator.php:212-213 -- every M.2 drive was over-counted against the expansion lane budget. FIXED at the catalog level (form_factor check mirroring :212-213 exactly, strpos 'm.2'/'m2' case-insensitive) -- NOT in DataExtractionUtilities::extractStoragePCIeLanes() itself, since legacy callers of that method depend on its current behavior. New assertions: tests/unit/resource_catalog_test.php (M.2 NVMe consumes() == [], U.2 NVMe still consumes 1 pcie_lane row amount=4, SATA == []) and tests/regression/ledger_dual_write_test.php (new scenario D2: M.2 storage add against a pre-seeded CPU pcie_lane budget writes NO consumption row; existing scenario D's fixture form_factor changed M.2->U.2 so it keeps proving the general consumption mechanism). Because this changes already-'verified' U-L.1/U-L.2 code, downgraded both back to 'implemented' and closed the PL gate pending a fresh independent verify pass -- per this repo's own convention, a session cannot re-verify its own fix to previously-verified code. OWNER DECISION EXECUTED (scratch-DB ghost-config cleanup, per the 2026-07-13 verify session's finding): confirmed via the live production read API that production carries exactly 9 configs; the scratch ims_compat_golden DB carried 12 (3 ghosts: 83f6849d 'Main Server', 9dda0039 'Production Servers', bbdd2549 'Testing Template' -- none have any config_components/config_resources/config_events rows, json-fallback-only, no inventory Status linkage to revert). Took a full mysqldump backup first (C:\tmp\ims-scratch-backups\ims_compat_golden_pre_ghost_delete_20260713.sql), deleted the 3 ghost config_uuids from server_configurations only (FK order: config_resources->config_events->config_components->server_configurations, all zero rows for these 3 except server_configurations itself), confirmed the remaining 9 scratch config_uuids match production's 9 exactly (set equality, not just count). PRODUCTION WAS NOT TOUCHED. Re-captured tests/golden/compatibility_baseline.json (12/93 -> 9/84): diffed old vs new, confirmed the ONLY change is the 3 ghost configs disappearing -- zero content drift on the 9 surviving configs. Re-ran fleet_parity_sweep.php: 84 replays/9 configs/0 threw, identical=47 expected=37 unexplained=0, GREEN (previously RED-by-design on bbdd2549's memory.type residue, now moot since that config no longer exists in the scratch mirror). Marked seeder database/seeders/2026_07_12_002_repair-bbdd2549-ddr4-to-ddr5-ram.sql SUPERSEDED via a header comment only (not deleted, not edited below the new comment, never run) -- its WHERE guards target a config production doesn't have, so running it would be a harmless no-op. FULL SWEEP: found and fixed a genuine (unrelated to RV-4) test-infrastructure bug while re-running all 30 tests/**/*_test.php files -- tests/backfill/ledger_backfill_test.php's runBackfill()/runFullScan() proc_open() env arrays omitted SystemRoot, which Windows' PDO mysql driver needs to resolve sockets; without it every backfill.php subprocess failed with a masquerading SQLSTATE[HY000][2002] 'DB unreachable' error even though the DB was fully reachable (confirmed via a standalone probe script: identical proc_open call succeeds once SystemRoot is added, fails identically without it). Fixed by adding SystemRoot (falls back to C:\Windows if unset) to both env arrays -- a 2-line, test-file-only change, no core/ code touched. Also cleaned up leftover throwaway inventory/config rows (SerialNumber LIKE '%-LG-%', config_uuid LIKE 'TEST-LGBF%') left behind by earlier failed runs of this same test from BEFORE the fix (their own --rollback-run cleanup couldn't reach the DB either) -- confirmed no config_components/config_resources/config_events/migration_backfill_state rows existed for them, only inventory + server_configurations, all synthetic test fixtures, none touching real fleet data. Final result: ALL 30 test files exit 0 (was 29/30 before the SystemRoot fix), fleet_parity_sweep GREEN unexplained=0, characterization ZERO drift against the freshly-recaptured 9/84 baseline. Production .env untouched throughout; ENGINE_MODE/STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED/COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED stayed off/unset; no seeder run; only the scratch ims_compat_golden DB was mutated (backed up first). Still open: Finding 2 (draft/building->finalized edge gap, unchanged), U-B.4/U-C.6 soak preconditions (unchanged, human/time-gated), U-D.1 plan unchanged and still blocked on P8+U-C.6 (migration/10-cleanup/U-D.1-PLAN-20260713.md), PL gate now closed pending independent re-verify of U-L.1/U-L.2 specifically (everything else in PL untouched).

fourth_session_2026_07_12.note: Owner picked SEC4 OPTION 2: ims-data/cpu/Cpu-details-level-3.json CPU 545e143b socket 'LGA 4189' -> 'LGA 4677' (factually correct for Sapphire Rapids Platinum 8480+), edited in both the canonical ims-data/ and its C:\tmp\ims-data scratch mirror. Fleet sweep re-run confirmed exactly the predicted outcome: all 5 cpu.socket_match diffs vanished (52 identical, up from 51), bbdd2549's previously-shadowed memory.type diff surfaced (4 unexplained, all on bbdd2549) -- DOCUMENTED with the config's full contents (DDR4 UDIMM non-ECC RAM 897472c6 on a DDR5-ECC-only X13DRG-H board) and NOT auto-resolved, per instruction; sweep stays RED by design pending an owner pick between an expected_diffs entry and a config-repair seeder. Built 3 in-transaction DB fixture batches that were previously fixture-gated SKIPs: (1) remove_command_test.php's rows-path motherboard blocked/cascade scenario (real motherboard+cpu inventory rows inserted as config_components rows inside the test's own rolled-back transaction); (2) replace_command_test.php's board A(LGA3647)->B(LGA2011-3) stranding scenario (blocks on cpu.socket_match, old board confirmed still live post-dryRun) and NIC-with-SFP re-anchor scenario (dependency.blocked_removal confirmed NOT firing once a motherboard+chassis presence fixture was added to isolate the signal from an unrelated structural-orphan requirement); (3) finalize_command_test.php's two-connection concurrency scenarios using two genuinely separate PDO connections against throwaway configs this session created and explicitly tore down (real commits, not rollbacks, since a second connection can't observe an uncommitted mutation or be blocked by a lock the first hasn't taken) -- 409 real-revision-after-concurrent-mutation (conn1 really commits a RemoveComponentCommand bumping revision 0->1, conn2 with the stale pre-mutation revision gets CommandFailed('revision_mismatch',...,409)) and finalize-race-blocks-under-lock (conn1 holds server_configurations' FOR UPDATE lock uncommitted, conn2 with innodb_lock_wait_timeout=1 gets CommandFailed('command_exception',...) via BaseCommand's catch-all, then succeeds past the lock once conn1 releases it). FULL SWEEP RE-RUN: all 28 unit+rules+regression+api suites exit 0 (every previously-SKIPPED DB-backed scenario in the three touched files now runs for real); characterization regenerated vs checked-in baseline -- 10/12 overlapping configs byte-identical, the other 2 (9dda0039, bbdd2549) differ ONLY in the now-resolved 'CPU socket LGA 4189 incompatible...' issue text disappearing (expected fallout of this session's own spec fix, not drift), same 3 gone uuids as every prior session, baseline RESTORED byte-for-byte and diffed clean against the real repo's untouched copy; fleet_parity_sweep reproducible across two independent runs (93 replays, 0 threw, identical=52 expected=37 unexplained=4, all memory.type on bbdd2549). tests/api/*'s remaining DB-backed criteria (golden response-shape, 409-over-real-HTTP, serial-targeted remove, two v2 actions) are unconditionally SKIPPED in the test file itself -- genuinely need a live HTTP context with CommandLayer::mode() !== 'off', which production rules forbid enabling, so left untouched and flagged rather than worked around. Everything this session: implemented, never self-certified verified. Production .env untouched; ENGINE_MODE/STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED/COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED stayed off/unset throughout. Still open: bbdd2549 memory.type owner decision (P5 gate blocker), Finding 2 (draft/building->finalized edge gap, unchanged), chassis A->B bay revalidation fixture (not attempted), tests/api/*'s live-HTTP-gated criteria, U-C.6/U-B.4/P2/P3 soak preconditions (unchanged, human/time-gated).

MID-SESSION TOOLING OUTAGE (honest account): after the above, Bash/PowerShell command execution (spawning php.exe/mysql.exe specifically; shell builtins kept working) became unavailable for the rest of the session despite dozens of retries over ~30 minutes, never recovering. As a direct result: the chassis fixture above was NOT run (design-reviewed by hand only); the full sweep re-run (all unit/rule/regression/api suites + fleet_parity_sweep.php) was NOT performed this session -- the last CONFIRMED full-green sweep remains the FOURTH session's (28/28 suites exit 0, identical=52/expected=37/unexplained=4).
